{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|越中国}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buqqhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|越中国}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|越中国}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|越中国}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|越中国}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:11]] It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. revision.comment: Text replacement - "|豊受大神}}" to "|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|越中国}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:11]] It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:11]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:11]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:qq]] Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Shrine {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine
