{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Shrine revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|12=draft}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|12=draft}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|12=draft}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=draft}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=draft|12=draft}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=draft|12=draft}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=draft|12=draft}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|12=draft|12=draft}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=draft|12=draft}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=draft|12=draft}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=draft|12=draft}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|12=draft|12=draft}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=draft|12=draft}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|12=draft|12=draft}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories|}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=draft|12=draft}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=draft|12=draft}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=draft|12=draft}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|12=draft|12=draft}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=draft|12=draft}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine's former rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=draft|12=draft}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=draft|12=draft}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|12=draft|12=draft}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=draft|12=draft}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=draft|12=draft}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=draft|12=draft}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|12=draft|12=draft}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

revision.comment: 57 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:加茂神社 (射水市) up to rev 86425208
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 加茂神社 |画像=[[ファイル:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|下村加茂神社]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} |所在地= 富山県射水市加茂中部630 | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 44 | 緯度秒 = 31.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 137 |経度分 = 8 | 経度秒 = 31.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Toyama#Japan |祭神= 玉依姫命
賀茂建角身命
賀茂別雷命 |社格= 郷社 |創建=[[治暦]]2年（[[1066年]]） |本殿= 一間流造 |別名= 下村の宮 |例祭= 5月4日（加茂祭） |神事= 稚児舞・鰤分け神事・やんさんま祭り・御田植祭 }} '''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、[[富山県]][[射水市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[賀茂御祖神社]]領[[倉垣荘]]の惣社で、旧社格は郷社。一般には'''下村加茂神社'''と呼ばれる。祭神は[[玉櫛媛|玉依姫命]]、[[賀茂建角身命]]、[[賀茂別雷命]]。 == 歴史 == 社伝では、[[1066年]]（[[治暦]]2年）に京都の[[賀茂神社]]から勧請されたと伝える。[[1090年]]（[[寛治]]4年）、[[白河天皇|白河上皇]]の勅願により[[倉垣荘]]が越中国射水郡に設けられると、荘家がこの加茂神社に置かれた。境内に隣接する加茂遺跡で、鎌倉期の居館跡が発掘された。荘域拡大に伴い、[[射水郡]]内各所に賀茂神が勧請された。 [[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）[[5月1日]]、郷社に昇格した『目で見る 高岡・氷見・新湊の100年』（1993年11月27日、郷土出版社発行）163ページ。。 == 祭事（無形文化財） == 年間50回近くに及ぶ加茂神社の祭事は、荘園維持行事（勧農儀礼・貢納儀礼）の継承とおよび賀茂信仰にまつわる[[葵祭]]の移入という特徴がある。射水市加茂地区・倉垣小杉地区・柳瀬地区の3地区で祭礼を維持している。 主な祭事は次のとおり。 * [[1月1日]]：鰤分け神事（射水市指定[[無形民俗文化財]]） * [[5月4日]]：[[やんさんま祭り]]（[[富山県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|富山県指定無形民俗文化財]]） * 6月上卯日：[[御田植祭]]（富山県指定無形民俗文化財） * [[9月4日]]：[[加茂神社の稚児舞|稚児舞]]（「[[越中の稚児舞]]」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定） == 境内末社 == * 貴布禰社（きふねしゃ）-[[淤加美神|闇龗神]]を祀る。例祭は、8月1日 * 任海社（とみしゃ）-[[高龗神]]を祀る。例祭は、8月1日 * 稲穂神社（いなほじんじゃ）-[[天照皇大神]]と[[豊受大神]]を祀る。例祭は、9月16日 == 「やんさんま祭り」 == : 詳細は[[やんさんま祭り]]の項を参照 [[5月4日]]に行われるお祭り。「[[流鏑馬]]」（やぶさめ）が訛ったものだが、流鏑馬以外にも、「'''牛乗式'''」という全国でも唯一の神事が行われる他、いろいろな神事が行われる。 == 有形文化財 == * 「獅子頭」2体（射水市指定有形文化財） == 名所・旧跡 == * [[福王寺 (射水市)|福王寺]]（旧・加茂神社神宮寺） [[真言宗]]。木造[[不動明王]]立像（富山県指定有形文化財）・木造[[阿弥陀如来]]坐像（富山県指定有形文化財）・木造[[毘沙門天]]立像（富山県指定有形文化財）が安置。賀茂本地仏の[[聖観世音菩薩]]坐像も祭られる。 == 交通 == * [[あいの風とやま鉄道線]][[呉羽駅]]より車で約10分、[[小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|小杉駅]]より車で約15分 * [[北陸自動車道]][[富山西インターチェンジ]]より車で約15分、[[小杉インターチェンジ]]より車で約20分 * 駐車場：有り == 脚注 == {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かもしんしや いみすし}} [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:射水市の歴史]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:射水市の建築物]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} |所在地= 富山県射水市加茂中部630 | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 44 | 緯度秒 = 31.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 137 |経度分 = 8 | 経度秒 = 31.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Toyama#Japan |祭神= 玉依姫命
賀茂建角身命
賀茂別雷命 |社格= 郷社 |創建=[[治暦]]2年（[[1066年]]） |本殿= 一間流造 |別名= 下村の宮 |例祭= 5月4日（加茂祭） |神事= 稚児舞・鰤分け神事・やんさんま祭り・御田植祭 }} '''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、[[富山県]][[射水市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[賀茂御祖神社]]領[[倉垣荘]]の惣社で、旧社格は郷社。一般には'''下村加茂神社'''と呼ばれる。祭神は[[玉櫛媛|玉依姫命]]、[[賀茂建角身命]]、[[賀茂別雷命]]。 == 歴史 == 社伝では、[[1066年]]（[[治暦]]2年）に京都の[[賀茂神社]]から勧請されたと伝える。[[1090年]]（[[寛治]]4年）、[[白河天皇|白河上皇]]の勅願により[[倉垣荘]]が越中国射水郡に設けられると、荘家がこの加茂神社に置かれた。境内に隣接する加茂遺跡で、鎌倉期の居館跡が発掘された。荘域拡大に伴い、[[射水郡]]内各所に賀茂神が勧請された。 [[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）[[5月1日]]、郷社に昇格した『目で見る 高岡・氷見・新湊の100年』（1993年11月27日、郷土出版社発行）163ページ。。 == 祭事（無形文化財） == 年間50回近くに及ぶ加茂神社の祭事は、荘園維持行事（勧農儀礼・貢納儀礼）の継承とおよび賀茂信仰にまつわる[[葵祭]]の移入という特徴がある。射水市加茂地区・倉垣小杉地区・柳瀬地区の3地区で祭礼を維持している。 主な祭事は次のとおり。 * [[1月1日]]：鰤分け神事（射水市指定[[無形民俗文化財]]） * [[5月4日]]：[[やんさんま祭り]]（[[富山県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|富山県指定無形民俗文化財]]） * 6月上卯日：[[御田植祭]]（富山県指定無形民俗文化財） * [[9月4日]]：[[加茂神社の稚児舞|稚児舞]]（「[[越中の稚児舞]]」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定） == 境内末社 == * 貴布禰社（きふねしゃ）-[[淤加美神|闇龗神]]を祀る。例祭は、8月1日 * 任海社（とみしゃ）-[[高龗神]]を祀る。例祭は、8月1日 * 稲穂神社（いなほじんじゃ）-[[天照皇大神]]と[[豊受大神]]を祀る。例祭は、9月16日 == 「やんさんま祭り」 == : 詳細は[[やんさんま祭り]]の項を参照 [[5月4日]]に行われるお祭り。「[[流鏑馬]]」（やぶさめ）が訛ったものだが、流鏑馬以外にも、「'''牛乗式'''」という全国でも唯一の神事が行われる他、いろいろな神事が行われる。 == 有形文化財 == * 「獅子頭」2体（射水市指定有形文化財） == 名所・旧跡 == * [[福王寺 (射水市)|福王寺]]（旧・加茂神社神宮寺） [[真言宗]]。木造[[不動明王]]立像（富山県指定有形文化財）・木造[[阿弥陀如来]]坐像（富山県指定有形文化財）・木造[[毘沙門天]]立像（富山県指定有形文化財）が安置。賀茂本地仏の[[聖観世音菩薩]]坐像も祭られる。 == 交通 == * [[あいの風とやま鉄道線]][[呉羽駅]]より車で約10分、[[小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|小杉駅]]より車で約15分 * [[北陸自動車道]][[富山西インターチェンジ]]より車で約15分、[[小杉インターチェンジ]]より車で約20分 * 駐車場：有り == 脚注 == {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かもしんしや いみすし}} [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:射水市の歴史]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:射水市の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=draft|12=draft}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|12=draft|12=draft}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=draft|12=draft}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=draft|12=draft}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|12=draft|12=draft}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|12=draft|12=draft}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=draft|12=draft}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|12=draft|12=draft}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]
