{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu City|qq=}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shirakawa|qq=}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu District|qq=}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Matsuri|qq=}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 1|qq=}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabusame|qq=}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amida Nyorai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bishamonten|qq=}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kureha Station|qq=}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Station (Toyama)|qq=}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 1|qq=}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Matsuri|qq=}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 1|qq=}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabusame|qq=}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amida Nyorai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bishamonten|qq=}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kureha Station|qq=}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Station (Toyama)|qq=}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu City|qq=}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shirakawa|qq=}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu District|qq=}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Matsuri|qq=}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 1|qq=}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabusame|qq=}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amida Nyorai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bishamonten|qq=}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kureha Station|qq=}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Station (Toyama)|qq=}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabusame|qq=}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala|2=ja|3=不動明王|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amida Nyorai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bishamonten|qq=}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kureha Station|qq=}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Station (Toyama)|qq=}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu City|qq=}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shirakawa|qq=}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu District|qq=}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Matsuri|qq=}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 1|qq=}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabusame|qq=}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala|2=ja|3=不動明王|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amida Nyorai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bishamonten|qq=}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kureha Station|qq=}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Station (Toyama)|qq=}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 1|qq=}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabusame|qq=}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala|2=ja|3=不動明王|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amida Nyorai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bishamonten|qq=}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kureha Station|qq=}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Station (Toyama)|qq=}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Imizu City|ja|射水市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu City|qq=}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}}, a manor of the {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉櫛媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|ja|白河天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shirakawa|qq=}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District|ja|射水郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imizu District|qq=}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|ja|葵祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Matsuri|qq=}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 1|qq=}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|ja|9月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4|qq=}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Okami no Kami|ja|淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabusame|qq=}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala|2=ja|3=不動明王|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amida Nyorai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bishamonten|qq=}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kureha Station|qq=}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Station (Toyama)|qq=}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[Category:Shimomura Kamo Shrine]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

Held on {{ill|May 4|ja|5月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 4|qq=}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabusame|qq=}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala|2=ja|3=不動明王|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amida Nyorai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bishamonten|qq=}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|ja|呉羽駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kureha Station|qq=}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Toyama)|ja|小杉駅 (富山県射水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Station (Toyama)|qq=}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]]

Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]]

Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:射水市の建築物]] [[Category:射水市の歴史]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:射水市の歴史]]
