Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 聖観音}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]]

Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 聖観音}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Imizu City]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Imizu City]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]]

Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 聖観音}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of {{ill|Shō Kannon|ja|聖観世音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 聖観音}}, a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]]

Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:下村加茂神社.jpg|270px|Shimomura Kamo Shrine]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|36|44|31.6|N|137|8|31.2|E}}}} | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|qid=Q851411}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]]}} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Imizu)]] [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|qid=Q851411}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|qid=Q851411}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}}
