Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|qq=|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}}

[[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|WD=Q851411}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|qq=|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|WD=Q851411}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}}

Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}}

[[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|WD=Q851411}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|WD=Q851411}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|WD=Q851411}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|WD=Q851411}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226778]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|WD=Q851411}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Imizu|Imizu City]], Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine]]. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in [[:en:1066|1066]] (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]. In [[:en:1090|1090]] (Kanji 4), [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout [[:en:Imizu District, Toyama|Imizu District]]. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Aoi Matsuri|Aoi Matsuri]]. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:May 4|May 4]]: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * [[:en:September 4|September 4]]: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami no Kami]]; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on [[:en:May 4|May 4]], this festival features a unique [[:en:Yabusame|Yabusame]] ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple housing statues of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from [[:en:Kureha Station|Kureha Station]] or 15 minutes from [[:en:Kosugi Station (Imizu)|Kosugi Station (Toyama)]]. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Held on {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|WD=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}, {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|WD=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|en|Kureha Station|WD=Q5359643|lt=Kureha Station}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|en|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|WD=Q5359711|lt=Kosugi Station (Toyama)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|WD=Q851411}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|WD=Q851411|lt=Imizu City}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|en|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamayorihime}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in {{ill|1066|en|1066|WD=Q19411|lt=1066}} (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine}}. In {{ill|1090|en|1090|WD=Q19466|lt=1090}} (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|en|Emperor Shirakawa|WD=Q60949|lt=Emperor Shirakawa}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District, Toyama|en|Imizu District, Toyama|WD=Q6004195|lt=Imizu District}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|en|Aoi Matsuri|WD=Q773085|lt=Aoi Matsuri}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|en|September 4|WD=Q2855|lt=September 4}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Okami no Kami}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|WD=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}, {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|WD=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|en|Kureha Station|WD=Q5359643|lt=Kureha Station}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|en|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|WD=Q5359711|lt=Kosugi Station (Toyama)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
