These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|en|September 4|WD=Q2855|lt=September 4}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Okami no Kami}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|WD=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}, {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|WD=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|en|Kureha Station|WD=Q5359643|lt=Kureha Station}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|en|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|WD=Q5359711|lt=Kosugi Station (Toyama)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|WD=Q851411}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|WD=Q851411|lt=Imizu City}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|en|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamayorihime}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in {{ill|1066|en|1066|WD=Q19411|lt=1066}} (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine}}. In {{ill|1090|en|1090|WD=Q19466|lt=1090}} (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|en|Emperor Shirakawa|WD=Q60949|lt=Emperor Shirakawa}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District, Toyama|en|Imizu District, Toyama|WD=Q6004195|lt=Imizu District}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|en|Aoi Matsuri|WD=Q773085|lt=Aoi Matsuri}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|en|September 4|WD=Q2855|lt=September 4}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Okami no Kami}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|WD=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Fukuōji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}, {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|WD=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|en|Kureha Station|WD=Q5359643|lt=Kureha Station}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|en|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|WD=Q5359711|lt=Kosugi Station (Toyama)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|WD=Q851411|lt=Imizu City|1=Imizu}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|en|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamayorihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in {{ill|1066|en|1066|WD=Q19411|lt=1066|1=1066}} (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}}. In {{ill|1090|en|1090|WD=Q19466|lt=1090|1=1090}} (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|en|Emperor Shirakawa|WD=Q60949|lt=Emperor Shirakawa|1=Emperor Shirakawa}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District, Toyama|en|Imizu District, Toyama|WD=Q6004195|lt=Imizu District|1=Imizu District, Toyama}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|en|Aoi Matsuri|WD=Q773085|lt=Aoi Matsuri|1=Aoi Matsuri}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|en|September 4|WD=Q2855|lt=September 4|1=September 4}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Okami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|WD=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame|1=Yabusame}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Q11594018|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}, {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|WD=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten|1=Vaiśravaṇa}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|en|Kureha Station|WD=Q5359643|lt=Kureha Station|1=Kureha Station}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|en|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|WD=Q5359711|lt=Kosugi Station (Toyama)|1=Kosugi Station (Imizu)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|WD=Q851411|lt=Imizu City|1=Imizu}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|en|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamayorihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in {{ill|1066|en|1066|WD=Q19411|lt=1066|1=1066}} (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}}. In {{ill|1090|en|1090|WD=Q19466|lt=1090|1=1090}} (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|en|Emperor Shirakawa|WD=Q60949|lt=Emperor Shirakawa|1=Emperor Shirakawa}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District, Toyama|en|Imizu District, Toyama|WD=Q6004195|lt=Imizu District|1=Imizu District, Toyama}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|en|Aoi Matsuri|WD=Q773085|lt=Aoi Matsuri|1=Aoi Matsuri}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|en|September 4|WD=Q2855|lt=September 4|1=September 4}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Okami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|WD=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame|1=Yabusame}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Q11594018|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}, {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|WD=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten|1=Vaiśravaṇa}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|en|Kureha Station|WD=Q5359643|lt=Kureha Station|1=Kureha Station}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|en|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|WD=Q5359711|lt=Kosugi Station (Toyama)|1=Kosugi Station (Imizu)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District, Toyama|en|Imizu District, Toyama|WD=Q6004195|lt=Imizu District|1=Imizu District, Toyama}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|en|Aoi Matsuri|WD=Q773085|lt=Aoi Matsuri|1=Aoi Matsuri}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|en|September 4|WD=Q2855|lt=September 4|1=September 4}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Okami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|WD=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame|1=Yabusame}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Q11594018|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}, {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|WD=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten|1=Vaiśravaṇa}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|en|Kureha Station|WD=Q5359643|lt=Kureha Station|1=Kureha Station}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|en|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|WD=Q5359711|lt=Kosugi Station (Toyama)|1=Kosugi Station (Imizu)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Buildings in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|WD=Q851411|lt=Imizu City|1=Imizu}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386607}}, a manor of the {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|en|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamayorihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in {{ill|1066|en|1066|WD=Q19411|lt=1066|1=1066}} (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}}. In {{ill|1090|en|1090|WD=Q19466|lt=1090|1=1090}} (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|en|Emperor Shirakawa|WD=Q60949|lt=Emperor Shirakawa|1=Emperor Shirakawa}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District, Toyama|en|Imizu District, Toyama|WD=Q6004195|lt=Imizu District|1=Imizu District, Toyama}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|en|Aoi Matsuri|WD=Q773085|lt=Aoi Matsuri|1=Aoi Matsuri}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|en|September 4|WD=Q2855|lt=September 4|1=September 4}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Okami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|WD=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame|1=Yabusame}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Q11594018|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}, {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|WD=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten|1=Vaiśravaṇa}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|en|Kureha Station|WD=Q5359643|lt=Kureha Station|1=Kureha Station}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|en|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|WD=Q5359711|lt=Kosugi Station (Toyama)|1=Kosugi Station (Imizu)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|qid=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|qid=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|en|September 4|qid=Q2855|lt=September 4|1=September 4}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|qid=Q644201|lt=Okami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|qid=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|qid=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame|1=Yabusame}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Q11594018|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}, {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|qid=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten|1=Vaiśravaṇa}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|en|Kureha Station|qid=Q5359643|lt=Kureha Station|1=Kureha Station}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|en|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|qid=Q5359711|lt=Kosugi Station (Toyama)|1=Kosugi Station (Imizu)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:加茂神社_(射水市)]] [[ja:加茂神社 (射水市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226778}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kamo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:下村加茂神社.jpg | location = Kamo Nakabe 630, Imizu City, Toyama Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 36 | Latitude minutes = 44 | Latitude seconds = 31.6 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 8 | Longitude seconds = 31.2 | E or W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Toyama#Japan | deity = Tamayorihime no Mikoto
Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto
Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (local shrine) | consecration_year = [[Chireki]] 2 (1066) | Main hall = Ichigenryū-zukuri | Alias = Shimomura no Miya | festival = May 4 (Kamo Festival) | Rituals = Chigo Dance, Buri-Wake Ceremony, Yansanma Festival, Otaue Festival | location = {{ill|Imizu|qid=Q851411|1=Imizu}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.74211111|N|137.142|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|qid=Q851411|lt=Imizu City|1=Imizu}}, Toyama Prefecture. It served as the main shrine of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|ja|倉垣荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|1=kuragakinoshou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11386607}}, a manor of the {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|qid=Q701620|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}. The shrine's former Rank is classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Locally, it is often referred to as "Shimomura Kamo Shrine." The enshrined deities are {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|en|Tamakushi-hime|qid=Q11572681|lt=Tamayorihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qid=Q11634939|lt=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634943}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kamo Shrine was established in {{ill|1066|en|1066|qid=Q19411|lt=1066|1=1066}} (Chireki 2) following the transfer of deities from Kyoto's {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|qid=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}}. In {{ill|1090|en|1090|qid=Q19466|lt=1090|1=1090}} (Kanji 4), {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|en|Emperor Shirakawa|qid=Q60949|lt=Emperor Shirakawa|1=Emperor Shirakawa}} decreed the establishment of the {{ill|Kuragaki-shō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragaki-shō|qq=}} in the former {{ill|Echū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with Kamo Shrine serving as the manor's headquarters. Excavations at the adjacent Kamo archaeological site have uncovered a residence from the Kamakura period. With the expansion of the manor, other Kamo shrines were established throughout {{ill|Imizu District, Toyama|en|Imizu District, Toyama|qid=Q6004195|lt=Imizu District|1=Imizu District, Toyama}}. On [[May 1, 1945]], the shrine was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}.『A Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato over 100 Years』郷土出版社, November 27, 1993, p. 163. == Festivals (Intangible Cultural Properties) == Kamo Shrine holds nearly 50 annual festivals, continuing agrarian and tribute rituals related to manor maintenance and incorporating influences from {{ill|Kamo Faith|ja|賀茂信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|en|Aoi Matsuri|qid=Q773085|lt=Aoi Matsuri|1=Aoi Matsuri}}. These traditions are maintained by three communities: Kamo, Kuragaki-Kosugi, and Yanase. Main festivals include: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|qid=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}}: {{ill|Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja|鰤分け神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buri-Wake Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated as Imizu City Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|qid=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}: {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * June Upper Zodiac Day: {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (Designated as Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * {{ill|September 4|en|September 4|qid=Q2855|lt=September 4|1=September 4}}: {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|加茂神社の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} (Part of the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Chigo Dance of Etchū|ja|越中の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Etchū|qq=}}) == Sub-Shrines == * Kifune Shrine (祇布禰社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|qid=Q644201|lt=Okami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}}; festival: August 1 * Tomi Shrine (任海社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|lt=Takami no Kami|ja|高龗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}; festival: August 1 * Inaho Shrine (稲穂神社) – Dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}}; festival: September 16 == Yansanma Festival == For details, see {{ill|Yansanma Festival|ja|やんさんま祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yansanma Festival|qq=}}. Held on {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|qid=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}, this festival features a unique {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|qid=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame|1=Yabusame}} ritual known as the "Bull Riding Ceremony," the only one of its kind in Japan, alongside other rituals. == Tangible Cultural Properties == * Two "Lion Heads" (Imizu City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties) == Notable Sites == * {{ill|Fukuōji Temple|ja|福王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōji Temple|1=Q11594018|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11594018}} (Former Shrine Temple of Kamo Shrine) A {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple housing statues of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}, {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|qid=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten|1=Vaiśravaṇa}}, all designated as Toyama Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties. The seated statue of [[:en:Shō Kannon|Shō Kannon]], a sacred image of Kamo Shrine, is also enshrined. == Access == * By car: Approximately 10 minutes from {{ill|Kureha Station|en|Kureha Station|qid=Q5359643|lt=Kureha Station|1=Kureha Station}} or 15 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|en|Kosugi Station (Imizu)|qid=Q5359711|lt=Kosugi Station (Toyama)|1=Kosugi Station (Imizu)}}. * By expressway: About 15 minutes from {{ill|Toyama-Nishi Interchange|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama-Nishi Interchange|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10954161}} or 20 minutes from {{ill|Kosugi Interchange|ja|小杉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Interchange|1=Kosugi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10959088}}. * Parking: Available. == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|version=86425208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q17226778|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|ja|加茂神社_(射水市)}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Imizu, Toyama]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
